label: Capabilities
title: Capabilities
description: Core ICOS capabilities define how voice, reflection, profile intelligence, public discovery, trust boundaries, and unified workspace behavior operate as one governed product system.

page.title: Capabilities
page.description: ICOS capabilities are not isolated features. They are governed product capabilities that connect voice, reflective cognition, profile memory, discovery, trust, and unified interaction routing into one modular system.

sections.label: Voice Training
sections.title: Voice Training
sections.description: Voice is a core capability surface, not a decorative layer.
paragraphs: ICOS is designed to learn from governed voice input so interaction quality can become more personal, more continuous, and more faithful to the user’s real way of speaking and thinking.

This capability supports continuity across time because tone, cadence, emotional patterning, and verbal structure all become part of the long-horizon model rather than being flattened into generic text utility.
Commercially, voice training shifts the product toward a more durable cognitive relationship, where the system can preserve identity signals instead of responding as an anonymous assistant.

sections.label: Reflection Loop
sections.title: Reflection Loop
sections.description: Capture, interpretation, return, and longitudinal reuse are one product loop.
paragraphs: ICOS turns raw thoughts, voice captures, questions, and emotional fragments into a structured reflection loop that can be revisited and improved over time.
The capability is not limited to single-turn answering. It exists to help the user see what is recurring, what is changing, and which patterns deserve long-term attention.
That loop gives the platform product depth because every interaction can contribute to better future understanding instead of disappearing after one reply.

sections.label: Profile Intelligence
sections.title: Profile Intelligence
sections.description: The profile is an intelligence layer, not a static account form.
paragraphs: Profile intelligence organizes identity fields, training progress, route readiness, permissions, and continuity maturity into one governed surface.
This capability matters because ICOS becomes more valuable as the profile becomes more coherent, more trusted, and more expressive of the real person behind it.
The result is a product where the profile record is itself part of the value proposition rather than a thin settings surface around the real system.

sections.label: Discovery & Selection
sections.title: Discovery & Selection
sections.description: Public models must be searchable, legible, and directly selectable.
paragraphs: ICOS is built to support searchable public continuity models so visitors can discover a profile by display name, username, description, tags, and identity signals.
Selection is a real routing capability. Once a model is chosen, the interaction engine, route prelude, and continuity context should follow that model rather than only changing a visual label.
This capability expands the platform from a private tool into a public interaction ecosystem where trusted profiles can be found and engaged intentionally.

sections.label: Verification & Permissions
sections.title: Verification & Permissions
sections.description: Trust and permissions are product capabilities, not afterthoughts.
paragraphs: Public safety, limited visibility, discoverability, creator attribution, and verification all need to be legible at the product level if public continuity is going to be commercially serious.
ICOS therefore treats route permission, voice permission, interaction permission, and legacy boundaries as part of the core capability set.
This creates a platform where trust is structurally expressed instead of being implied by marketing language.

sections.label: Unified Workspace
sections.title: Unified Workspace
sections.description: Search, settings, continuity, and profile controls belong to one governed interaction shell.
paragraphs: The homepage and private surfaces should behave like one modular workspace where search, continuity, settings, and identity are separate systems but part of one coherent shell.
That shell is what makes ICOS feel like a product system rather than a collection of disconnected panels and pages.
As the platform matures, this capability becomes the surface where voice entry, profile routing, trust state, and continuity navigation remain unified instead of fragmented.
